perturbation.original_text: Information does not include confidential informa tion that: (i) is or becomes generally known and available to the public other than as a result of any breach by Company of this NDA; (ii) is subsequently learned from a third party who is under no obligation of confidentiality with respect to such Information; (iii) wa s known by Company under no obligation of confidentiality prior to disclosure by the State Bar he reunder; or (iv) was generated independently by Company without reference to the State Bar’s Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Information does not include confidential informa tion that: (i) is or becomes generally known and available to the public other than as a direct result of a breach by Company, and such breach is proven with substantial evidence, of this NDA; (ii) is subsequently learned from a third party who is under no obligation of confidentiality with respect to such Information; (iii) wa s known by Company under no obligation of confidentiality prior to disclosure by the State Bar he reunder; or (iv) was generated independently by Company without reference to the State Bar’s Information.
perturbation.explanation: By adding the requirement of 'direct result' and 'proven with substantial evidence' to the exception regarding information becoming publicly known, we introduce a contradiction. Later in the NDA, the clause stating 'Company's confidentiality obligations hereunder will be conti nuing and will survive expiration or termination of the NDA' is now contradicted. The added conditionality makes it harder to determine when information is no longer confidential, making the surviving confidentiality obligation ambiguous and harder to enforce, creating an in-text contradiction, since the confidentiality can be compromised.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Company's confidentiality oblig ations hereunder will be conti nuing and will survive expiration or termination of the NDA.
perturbation.justification: The modified text adds the requirements of 'direct result' and 'proven with substantial evidence' to the exception regarding information becoming publicly known. This introduces conditionality that contradicts the clause stating 'Company's confidentiality obligations hereunder will be continuing and will survive expiration or termination of the NDA'. The added conditionality makes it harder to determine when information is no longer confidential, making the surviving confidentiality obligation ambiguous and harder to enforce, creating an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Company will use its best efforts to ensure th at such individuals adhere to the NDA, and Company agrees that it will be responsible for any breach of this NDA by any of its employees, agents, authorized representatives or permitted assignees or subcontractors.
perturbation.changed_text: Company will use its best efforts to ensure th at such individuals adhere to the NDA, and Company agrees that it will only be responsible for any willful breach of this NDA by any of its direct employees.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'any breach' to 'any willful breach' and limiting responsibility to 'direct employees' creates a conflict. This contradicts the clause about the State Bar being entitled to 'specific performance, injunctive relief or any other eq uitable remedy', because now if an agent or subcontractor negligently breaches the NDA, the State Bar's ability to seek those remedies is undermined since the Company is not responsible for the actions of these individuals.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Company acknowledges and agrees that the reme dy at law for any breach of this NDA is inadequate and that, in additi on to monetary damages, including but not limited to special, incidental, consequential or puniti ve damages, and any other avai lable relief at law, whether based in contract, tort or otherwise, the State Bar will be entitled to specific performance, injunctive relief or any other eq uitable remedy without the need to (i) prove actual damages and/or (ii) post any bond or other security deposit in connection with such remedy.
perturbation.justification: The original text made the company responsible for 'any breach' of the NDA by its employees, agents, representatives, assignees, or subcontractors, while the changed text limits responsibility to 'any willful breach' by 'direct employees' only. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text', which states that the State Bar is entitled to 'specific performance, injunctive relief or any other equitable remedy' for 'any breach' of the NDA. The change now creates a situation where the State Bar's ability to seek those remedies is undermined if an agent or subcontractor negligently breaches the NDA because the Company is no longer responsible for those actions. This aligns with the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition of the Perturbation Type.

perturbation.original_text: Company acknowledges and agrees that the reme dy at law for any breach of this NDA is inadequate and that, in additi on to monetary damages, including but not limited to special, incidental, consequential or puniti ve damages, and any other avai lable relief at law, whether based in contract, tort or otherwise, the State Bar will be entitled to specific performance, injunctive relief or any other eq uitable remedy without the need to (i) prove actual damages and/or (ii) post any bond or other security deposit in connection with such remedy.
perturbation.changed_text: Company acknowledges and agrees that the reme dy at law for any breach of this NDA is inadequate and that, in additi on to monetary damages, including but not limited to special, incidental, consequential or puniti ve damages, and any other avai lable relief at law, whether based in contract, tort or otherwise, the State Bar will only be entitled to monetary damages directly correlated to the breach, without the need to (i) prove actual damages and/or (ii) post any bond or other security deposit in connection with such remedy.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the State Bar's entitlement to remedies to only 'monetary damages directly correlated to the breach', we create a conflict. This contradicts the immediately following phrase stating 'the State Bar will be entitled to specific performance, injunctive relief or any other eq uitable remedy'. Now it is unclear if the state bar is entitiled to any other remedies besides direct monetary damages.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Company acknowledges and agrees that the reme dy at law for any breach of this NDA is inadequate and that, in additi on to monetary damages, including but not limited to special, incidental, consequential or puniti ve damages, and any other avai lable relief at law, whether based in contract, tort or otherwise, the State Bar will only be entitled to monetary damages directly correlated to the breach, without the need to (i) prove actual damages and/or (ii) post any bond or other security deposit in connection with such remedy.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original text, limiting the State Bar's remedies to 'monetary damages directly correlated to the breach,' while the original text and contradicted text state that the State Bar is entitled to 'specific performance, injunctive relief or any other equitable remedy'. This creates a direct contradiction within the same section of the contract, regarding the remedies available to the State Bar, thus creating an Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction, because two different sections of the contract provide conflicting remedies.

perturbation.original_text: This NDA will be governed and constr ued in all respects in accordan ce with the laws of the State of California without regard to its conflict of laws principles.
perturbation.changed_text: This NDA will be governed and constr ued in all respects in accordan ce with the laws of the State of California, except for matters relating to intellectual property which will be governed by federal law, without regard to its conflict of laws principles.
perturbation.explanation: Introducing the exception of 'matters relating to intellectual property' being governed by federal law creates a conflict. Earlier in the NDA, it is stated that 'All Information disclosed by the State Bar is and at all times will remain the sole and exclusiv e property of the State Ba r.' The modified text creates an ambiguity regarding which law applies if the confidential information is considered intellectual property, thus creating in-text contradiction on whether state or federal laws are applied.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All Information disclosed by the State Bar is and at all times will remain the sole and exclusiv e property of the State Ba r.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the NDA is governed by the laws of California. The changed text introduces an exception for matters relating to intellectual property, which will be governed by federal law. The contradicted text states that all information disclosed by the State Bar is the exclusive property of the State Bar. This creates a contradiction because if the disclosed information is considered intellectual property, it is unclear whether California law or federal law applies, creating in-text contradiction regarding which laws apply.

perturbation.original_text: Company agrees that it will reta in all Information provided in connection with the RFP in the strictest confidence, and will neither use nor di sclose such Information to anyone other than those who, in each case, need to know the Information for the purpose of responding to this RFP.
perturbation.changed_text: Company agrees that it will reta in all Information provided in connection with the RFP in the strictest confidence, and will neither use nor di sclose such Information to anyone other than those who, in each case, need to know the Information for the purpose of responding to this RFP, or for internal research purposes.
perturbation.explanation: By adding 'or for internal research purposes' to the exceptions for disclosure, a contradiction is created. This directly conflicts with the statement 'use any Info rmation to access or obtain additional information outside the purpose contemplated by this NDA.' Using the information for internal research goes beyond simply responding to the RFP, thereby creating an ambiguity in the enforcement, by introducing the internal research exception.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Without the State Bar’s prior written consent, Company will not: (i) disclose to any person or entity the substance or import of the Information; (ii) use any Info rmation to access or obtain additional information outside the purpose contemplated by this NDA; (iii) duplicate, di stribute or otherwise reproduce any Information in any way.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds 'or for internal research purposes' to the exceptions for disclosing information, directly contradicting the contradicted text, which states the company will not 'use any Information to access or obtain additional information outside the purpose contemplated by this NDA.' The addition of internal research goes beyond responding to the RFP, creating a clear conflict and uncertainty.

perturbation.original_text: Upon submission of a proposal to the RFP, or if the State Bar so requests, whichever occurs first, Company will promptly, and at its own expense: (i ) redeliver to the State Bar all copies of the Information in Company’s possession, whether such Information is in written, electronic, digital or other form or format; (ii) destroy any a nd all analyses, compilations, studies, or other documents in any form or format that were prep ared by or for the use of Company which contain or reflect any Information; and (iii) certify such destruction to the State Bar in writing by an authorized officer of the Company who supervised such destruction, as well as the return of all copies as specified above.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon submission of a proposal to the RFP, or if the State Bar so requests with a 30-day written notice, whichever occurs first, Company will promptly, and at its own expense: (i ) redeliver to the State Bar all copies of the Information in Company’s possession, whether such Information is in written, electronic, digital or other form or format; (ii) destroy any a nd all analyses, compilations, studies, or other documents in any form or format that were prep ared by or for the use of Company which contain or reflect any Information; and (iii) certify such destruction to the State Bar in writing by an authorized officer of the Company who supervised such destruction, as well as the return of all copies as specified above.
perturbation.explanation: By adding 'with a 30-day written notice' to the condition where 'if the State Bar so requests' a contradiction is formed. Originally it says 'whichever occurs first', but now the 'State Bar so requests' has a prior step of 30 day notification. If Submission of Proposal occurs before 30 days, what condition occurs first? This introduces ambiguity regarding which deadline takes precedence for the return of confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Upon submission of a proposal to the RFP, or if the State Bar so requests with a 30-day written notice, whichever occurs first, Company will promptly, and at its own expense: (i ) redeliver to the State Bar all copies of the Information in Company’s possession, whether such Information is in written, electronic, digital or other form or format; (ii) destroy any a nd all analyses, compilations, studies, or other documents in any form or format that were prep ared by or for the use of Company which contain or reflect any Information; and (iii) certify such destruction to the State Bar in writing by an authorized officer of the Company who supervised such destruction, as well as the return of all copies as specified above.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a 30-day written notice requirement for the State Bar's request. This contradicts the original 'whichever occurs first' clause, creating ambiguity. The submission of a proposal and the State Bar's request with a 30-day notice are now potentially conflicting triggers, as highlighted in the perturbation explanation. This ambiguity aligns with the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' because different sections of a contract provide conflicting deadlines.
